etymology.description: Depicts a jade artifact with carved patterns -> borrowed as a country name -> 口 (mouth) added for distinction -> top part evolved into 用 (use)
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicting a jade artifact with intricate carvings, this character was borrowed to represent a geographical area. The addition of 口 (kǒu, "mouth") served as a distinguishing mark over time. The meaning of 'circle' or 'complete cycle' comes from the idea of encircling and encompassing something entirely, akin to the completeness or intricacy of the jade artifact, leading to senses of wholeness and completion.

examples.chinese: 他们每周都去游泳(他們每週都去游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen měi zhōu dōu qù yóuyǒng.

examples.chinese: 他在报告中提出了完整的解决方案(他在報告中提出了完整的解決方案)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài bàogào zhōng tíchū le wánzhěng de jiějué fāng'àn.
examples.english: He provided a complete solution in the report.

examples.chinese: 春天到来时(春天到來時)，我们可以看到生命的循环(我們可以看到生命的循環)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān dàolái shí, wǒmen kěyǐ kàn dào shēngmìng de xúnhuán.
examples.english: When spring arrives, we can see the cycle of life.
